chunks.text: A small concert was held in the town hall.
Whoever came could enter with free admission.
Whoever performed was warmly welcomed by the audience.
The first to perform was a girl of ten, holding a violin.
When she finished, however small the note, it reached every heart in the room.
Whichever movement she played, her tone was pure.
Whoever listened could not help but admire the purity of her sound.
That evening, the hall was more peaceful than I had ever seen
and people exchanged smile after smile.
This kind of place, whenever it exists
is the small heritage that I want to treasure.
